69d0a6d92edcce58c88e9353.question: An artist from this movement painted his wife Effie Gray in Peace Concluded. A group of models from this movement were called the “Six Stunners.” An artist from this movement painted a lone weaver in a red dress who is “Half-Sick of Shadows.” That artist, who often depicted the Lady of Shalott, is (*) John William Waterhouse. An artist from this movement used Elizabeth Siddal to model a woman floating in a river in his painting Ophelia. For 10 points, John Everett Millais cofounded what British art movement that rejected the influence of an Italian painter?
69d0a6d92edcce58c88e9353.question_sanitized: An artist from this movement painted his wife Effie Gray in Peace Concluded. A group of models from this movement were called the "Six Stunners." An artist from this movement painted a lone weaver in a red dress who is "Half-Sick of Shadows." That artist, who often depicted the Lady of Shalott, is (*) John William Waterhouse. An artist from this movement used Elizabeth Siddal to model a woman floating in a river in his painting Ophelia. For 10 points, John Everett Millais cofounded what British art movement that rejected the influence of an Italian painter?

69d0a6da2edcce58c88e938b.leadin: A fifteen-line poem named for one of these figures describes a “broken wall” and “burning roof and tower.” For 10 points each:
69d0a6da2edcce58c88e938b.leadin_sanitized: A fifteen-line poem named for one of these figures describes a "broken wall" and "burning roof and tower." For 10 points each:
parts: Name these animals, which the speaker counts “nine-and-fifty” of in another poem set in Coole Park.
Both “Leda and the Swan” and “The Wild Swans at Coole” are poems by this author, who wrote about a “rough beast” slouching towards Bethlehem in “The Second Coming.”
Yeats’ poem “The Wild Swans at Coole” was set at an estate owned by this woman, whose son was also the subject of “An Irish Airman Foresees His Death.”
parts_sanitized: Name these animals, which the speaker counts "nine-and-fifty" of in another poem set in Coole Park.
Both "Leda and the Swan" and "The Wild Swans at Coole" are poems by this author, who wrote about a "rough beast" slouching towards Bethlehem in "The Second Coming."
Yeats' poem "The Wild Swans at Coole" was set at an estate owned by this woman, whose son was also the subject of "An Irish Airman Foresees His Death."
answers: swans [prompt on birds; accept “Leda and the Swan” or “The Wild Swans at Coole”]
Lady Gregory [accept Isabella Augusta, Lady Gregory or Isabella Augusta Persse]
answers_sanitized: swans [prompt on birds; accept "Leda and the Swan" or "The Wild Swans at Coole"]
Lady Gregory [accept Isabella Augusta, Lady Gregory or Isabella Augusta Persse]

69d0a6db2edcce58c88e940a.leadin: In one opera, a character laments her withered flowers in the aria “Ah! non credea mirarti” while performing this action. For 10 points each:
69d0a6db2edcce58c88e940a.leadin_sanitized: In one opera, a character laments her withered flowers in the aria "Ah! non credea mirarti" while performing this action. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this action that causes Elvino to mistakenly believe that Amina is having an affair with Rodolfo. In a Verdi opera, a queen laments not being able to cleanse her hands of blood during a scene nicknamed for this action.
Amina is the title “sleepwalker” of an opera by this composer. A druid priestess sings the aria “Casta diva” in this composer’s opera Norma.
Bellini depicted characters from this play as members of the Guelphs and Ghibellines in his opera The Capulets and the Montagues.
parts_sanitized: Name this action that causes Elvino to mistakenly believe that Amina is having an affair with Rodolfo. In a Verdi opera, a queen laments not being able to cleanse her hands of blood during a scene nicknamed for this action.
Amina is the title "sleepwalker" of an opera by this composer. A druid priestess sings the aria "Casta diva" in this composer's opera Norma.
Bellini depicted characters from this play as members of the Guelphs and Ghibellines in his opera The Capulets and the Montagues.
answers: sleepwalking [or somnambulism; or noctambulism; accept La Sonnambula or The Sleepwalker; accept the sleepwalking scene (in Macbeth); prompt on sleeping; prompt on walking]
answers_sanitized: sleepwalking [or somnambulism; or noctambulism; accept La Sonnambula or The Sleepwalker; accept the sleepwalking scene (in Macbeth); prompt on sleeping; prompt on walking]

69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e9493.question: In 2024, Victoria Thomas Bowen threw a milkshake at this party’s leader on his first day of campaigning. Elon Musk’s alleged $100 million donation to this party in December 2024 was ridiculed as “pure speculation” in a BBC report. A Guardian article criticized this party’s leader for accusing immigrants of (*) eating swans from The Royal Parks. This party, whose first MP was Lee Anderson, has the slogan “Britain is broken, Britain needs [this party’s name].” For 10 points, name this right-wing populist party that is currently led by Nigel Farage.
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e9493.question_sanitized: In 2024, Victoria Thomas Bowen threw a milkshake at this party's leader on his first day of campaigning. Elon Musk's alleged $100 million donation to this party in December 2024 was ridiculed as "pure speculation" in a BBC report. A Guardian article criticized this party's leader for accusing immigrants of (*) eating swans from The Royal Parks. This party, whose first MP was Lee Anderson, has the slogan "Britain is broken, Britain needs [this party's name]." For 10 points, name this right-wing populist party that is currently led by Nigel Farage.
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e9493.answer: Reform UK [accept the Brexit Party; accept “Britain is broken, Britain needs Reform”]
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e9493.answer_sanitized: Reform UK [accept the Brexit Party; accept "Britain is broken, Britain needs Reform"]

69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e94b8.question: The words “e van!” are repeated by a character in this opera to end an F-sharp major “Ballatella.” A character in this opera exclaims, “Name of God! Those same words!” after hearing another character declare “I will always be yours!” In this opera’s prologue, the audience is instructed to “mark well…our souls” by a character who asks “Si può?” After stabbing (*) Nedda and Silvio, a character in this opera declares “La commedia è finita!” Canio sings “Vesti la giubba” in, for 10 points, what Ruggero Leoncavallo opera about a troupe of clowns?
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e94b8.question_sanitized: The words "e van!" are repeated by a character in this opera to end an F-sharp major "Ballatella." A character in this opera exclaims, "Name of God! Those same words!" after hearing another character declare "I will always be yours!" In this opera's prologue, the audience is instructed to "mark well...our souls" by a character who asks "Si puo?" After stabbing (*) Nedda and Silvio, a character in this opera declares "La commedia e finita!" Canio sings "Vesti la giubba" in, for 10 points, what Ruggero Leoncavallo opera about a troupe of clowns?

69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e94ef.question: Alexius Meinong developed a variant of this idea based on his unique theory of objects. Charles Hartshorne used modal logic to develop this idea, as did Alvin Plantinga. Gaunilo analogized this idea to one about a “perfect island” to refute it in On Behalf of the Fool. The (*) Proslogion advances this argument, which is based on the principle that a “being than which nothing greater can be conceived” exists. For 10 points, St. Anselm most notably presented what argument for the existence of God?
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e94ef.question_sanitized: Alexius Meinong developed a variant of this idea based on his unique theory of objects. Charles Hartshorne used modal logic to develop this idea, as did Alvin Plantinga. Gaunilo analogized this idea to one about a "perfect island" to refute it in On Behalf of the Fool. The (*) Proslogion advances this argument, which is based on the principle that a "being than which nothing greater can be conceived" exists. For 10 points, St. Anselm most notably presented what argument for the existence of God?
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e94ef.answer: ontological argument [prompt on arguments for the existence of God before mention]
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e94ef.answer_sanitized: ontological argument [prompt on arguments for the existence of God before mention]

69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e952f.leadin: Claims to “Let us swear to remain friends!” and “let nothing part us” are sung in this duet. For 10 points each:
69d0a6dc2edcce58c88e952f.leadin_sanitized: Claims to "Let us swear to remain friends!" and "let nothing part us" are sung in this duet. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this duet. This act one duet is sung by Nadir and Zurga upon their reunion in Ceylon.
The lyrics “Come, Mallika, the flowering lianas already cast their shadow” opens “The Flower Duet”, which this composer included in his opera Lakmé.
Giulietta and Nicklausse sing a “Barcarolle” in this composer’s opera The Tales of Hoffmann. This man also composed the opera Orpheus in the Underworld.
parts_sanitized: Name this duet. This act one duet is sung by Nadir and Zurga upon their reunion in Ceylon.
The lyrics "Come, Mallika, the flowering lianas already cast their shadow" opens "The Flower Duet", which this composer included in his opera Lakme.
Giulietta and Nicklausse sing a "Barcarolle" in this composer's opera The Tales of Hoffmann. This man also composed the opera Orpheus in the Underworld.
answers: “Au fond du temple saint” [or “At the back of the holy temple”; accept “The Pearl Fishers Duet”]
answers_sanitized: "Au fond du temple saint" [or "At the back of the holy temple"; accept "The Pearl Fishers Duet"]
Leo Delibes [or Clement Philibert Leo Delibes]
